records.sprk_eventname: Complaint Filed — Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries
records.sprk_description: Filed patent infringement complaint in the Northern District of California, Case No. 3:2025-cv-04892. Alleges Pinnacle Industries AutoForge product line infringes three Meridian Corp precision manufacturing process patents.
Deadline for defendant Pinnacle Industries to file answer or responsive pleading to the complaint. Answer filed September 12, 2025, asserting invalidity and non-infringement defenses.
Both parties required to serve initial disclosures under Rule 26(a)(1). Meridian Corp served disclosures September 30, 2025 identifying key witnesses and documents.
Deadline for completion of all fact discovery including depositions, document production, and interrogatories. Parties working to complete remaining depositions before cutoff.
Deadline for plaintiff Meridian Corp to serve final expert reports. Dr. Robert Patel's initial report on manufacturing processes completed; rebuttal report preparation underway.
Deadline for filing summary judgment motions and Daubert motions. Team evaluating strength of summary judgment motion on infringement claims.
Final pre-trial conference before Hon. Margaret Sullivan. Parties to submit proposed jury instructions, witness lists, exhibit lists, and motions in limine.

records.sprk_eventname: Trial — Meridian Corp v. Pinnacle Industries
records.sprk_description: Jury trial scheduled for two weeks. Patent infringement and invalidity claims. Projected trial date may shift depending on settlement discussions and motion practice.

Deposition of James Morrison, VP of Engineering at Meridian Corp. Key fact witness who led the team that developed the patented precision manufacturing processes. Deposition completed over two days at Baker & Associates offices.

Deposition of Thomas Wright, CEO of Pinnacle Industries. Testified regarding AutoForge product development decisions, awareness of Meridian patents, and internal research processes. Deposition held at Chen Law Group offices in Austin.
Deposition of Dr. Robert Patel, plaintiff's expert witness on manufacturing processes. Scheduled for March 10, 2026. Preparation sessions with Dr. Patel underway.
Deposition of Dr. Emily Nakamura, defendant's expert on patent analysis and invalidity. Scheduled for March 20, 2026. Cross-examination preparation in progress.
Deposition of Amanda Foster, In-House Counsel at Pinnacle Industries. Testified regarding Pinnacle's IP portfolio management, awareness of Meridian patents, and document retention policies.

records.sprk_eventname: Document Review — Batch 1: Meridian Internal Correspondence
records.sprk_description: First batch review of Meridian Corp internal emails and memoranda relating to the patented manufacturing processes. Approximately 2,500 documents reviewed for relevance and privilege.

records.sprk_eventname: Document Review — Batch 2: Pinnacle Production Set A
records.sprk_description: Review of Pinnacle Industries first document production comprising AutoForge product design specifications, engineering notebooks, and internal communications. Approximately 4,100 documents.

records.sprk_eventname: Document Review — Batch 3: Patent Prosecution History
records.sprk_description: Review of complete prosecution history for the three asserted patents, including office actions, applicant responses, and examiner interviews. Critical for claim construction arguments.

records.sprk_eventname: Privilege Log Review — Meridian Withheld Documents
records.sprk_description: Preparation and review of privilege log for Meridian Corp withheld documents. 342 documents logged as attorney-client privilege or work product. Log served with second supplemental production.

records.sprk_eventname: Document Review — Batch 4: Pinnacle Production Set B
records.sprk_description: Review of Pinnacle's supplemental document production including source code excerpts, testing data, and manufacturing process specifications for AutoForge. Approximately 3,200 documents.

records.sprk_eventname: Document Review — Batch 5: Third-Party Subpoena Responses
records.sprk_description: Review of document productions received from third-party subpoena respondents including component suppliers and industry trade association records. Approximately 800 documents.

records.sprk_eventname: Document Review — Batch 6: ESI Forensic Collection
records.sprk_description: Review of electronically stored information collected through forensic imaging of key Pinnacle custodian devices. Includes deleted files and metadata analysis. 1,600 documents processed.

records.sprk_eventname: Privilege Log Review — Pinnacle Withheld Documents
records.sprk_description: Analysis of Pinnacle's privilege log to identify potential clawback challenges. Identified 15 entries with questionable privilege assertions for possible motion to compel.

records.sprk_eventname: Expert Report Review — Dr. Patel Initial Report
records.sprk_description: Internal review of Dr. Patel's initial expert report on manufacturing process infringement analysis. Report covers claim-by-claim comparison of Meridian patents versus AutoForge implementation. Approved with minor revisions.

records.sprk_eventname: Expert Report Review — Dr. Nakamura Invalidity Analysis
records.sprk_description: Review of defendant's expert Dr. Nakamura's invalidity report. Analysis focuses on prior art references and claim construction arguments. Team preparing rebuttal strategy.

records.sprk_eventname: Expert Report Review — Dr. Patel Rebuttal Draft
records.sprk_description: Review of Dr. Patel's draft rebuttal report responding to Dr. Nakamura's invalidity opinions. Draft currently under attorney review with comments due by March 10.
Review of financial discovery documents related to damages calculation including Pinnacle revenue data, market analysis, and licensing comparables. Approximately 1,100 documents.
Initial Rule 16 scheduling conference before Hon. Margaret Sullivan. Court set discovery deadlines, trial date, and referred parties to patent local rules. Case management order issued September 23.

Hearing on parties' dispute regarding ESI search protocol and custodian scope. Court largely adopted Meridian's proposed search terms with modifications. Pinnacle ordered to produce from 4 additional custodians.
Hearing on Meridian's motion to compel production of 12 documents Pinnacle withheld on privilege grounds. Court ordered in camera review of 8 documents and sustained privilege for 4.

Markman hearing on disputed claim terms for the three asserted patents. Court construed 6 of 8 disputed terms in Meridian's favor. Key term 'precision alignment mechanism' construed broadly, strengthening infringement position.

records.sprk_eventname: Motion Hearing — Motion to Compel Third-Party Subpoenas
records.sprk_description: Hearing on Meridian's motion to compel two third-party suppliers to comply with subpoenas. Court granted motion with limited scope, ordering production within 21 days.

Anticipated Daubert challenge to Dr. Patel's methodology on infringement analysis. Team preparing to defend expert's qualifications and methodology. Hearing date to be set after expert discovery closes.

records.sprk_eventname: Strategy Session — Case Assessment and Litigation Plan
records.sprk_description: Initial case strategy meeting with Rachel Torres, Michael Park, and client Sarah Chen. Established litigation objectives, budget framework, and discovery priorities. Identified key prior art risks and infringement theories.
Team meeting to finalize discovery strategy including custodian identification, search term development, and ESI protocol. Assigned document review responsibilities and set production timeline.

Internal discussion of settlement posture with Sarah Chen. Evaluated case strengths and weaknesses after initial discovery. Client authorized exploration of settlement range between $15M-$25M. No formal demand made yet.

Settlement strategy meeting following favorable Markman ruling. Discussed strengthened position and revised demand range. Client considering making formal settlement demand in March. Opposing party signaling willingness to discuss in Q2 2026.
Quarterly budget review with Sarah Chen. Reviewed initial $28K in legal fees from August. Discussed projected discovery costs and expert engagement timeline. Budget on track at 5.7% utilization.

Year-end budget review. Through December, cumulative spend is $205,190 representing 41% of $500K budget. Discovery costs tracking slightly above projections. Expert fees commencing in January. Recommended reallocation from Trial Prep to Discovery bucket.

Monthly budget check. Cumulative spend $317,550, representing 63.5% of budget at month 7 of 11. Expert fees trending higher than projected due to additional rebuttal work. Discussed contingency measures if settlement discussions fail.
Weekly team meeting — Rachel Torres, Michael Park, Lisa Huang. Reviewed case status, upcoming deadlines, and task assignments. Discussed initial discovery plan and document collection priorities.
Multi-day preparation session for Markman hearing. Finalized claim construction positions, prepared demonstratives, and rehearsed oral arguments. Dr. Patel consulted on technical claim terms.

Comprehensive case assessment as fact discovery nears completion. Team to evaluate strength of infringement case, damages theories, and settlement leverage. Will develop recommendations for trial preparation strategy.
Auto-generated reminder to supplement initial disclosures per Rule 26(e) obligation. Supplemental disclosures served November 28 identifying additional witnesses and correcting prior production.
Filed Meridian Corp's opening claim construction brief addressing 8 disputed claim terms. Brief supported by Dr. Patel's declaration on technical terminology and prosecution history analysis.

records.sprk_eventname: Filing — Opposition to Motion to Compel
records.sprk_description: Filed opposition to Pinnacle's motion to compel additional source code production. Argued production was overbroad and sought confidential trade secrets beyond the scope of the patents at issue.
Monthly status report to Meridian Corp covering October activities. Summarized Pinnacle's first production, deposition scheduling progress, and upcoming discovery dispute hearing on ESI protocol.

Monthly status report to Meridian Corp for December. Reported completion of Wright and Foster depositions, expert engagement progress, and year-end budget position. Flagged upcoming Markman hearing preparation.
Monthly status report to Meridian Corp for February. Reported favorable Markman ruling, expert report progress, upcoming depositions of both experts, and settlement posture analysis.
Auto-generated reminder for Meridian's responses to Pinnacle's first set of interrogatories. Responses verified and served on October 19.

Auto-generated reminder for expert discovery cutoff. All expert depositions and rebuttal reports must be completed by April 15, 2026. Plaintiff and defendant expert depositions currently scheduled for March.

Filed joint case management statement as required by court standing order. Reported discovery progress, remaining depositions, anticipated motions, and settlement status. Both parties confirmed trial date of June 30, 2026 remains viable.
